subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Clear and sustained focus on ethics as the key concept
Analytical links between ethical considerations and multiple business functions (SWOT, marketing, privacy)
Strong organizational connection with specific Temu data and events
weaknesses: Ethical lens not seamlessly woven into some quantitative sections (e.g., financial analysis)
Generic tool definitions (SWOT) lack explicit ethical framing
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place this work solidly in Band 4: the student shows strong understanding and clear analytical thinking around ethics, with sustained organization-specific connections. Full Band 5 integration is prevented by compartmentalized sections and occasional lapses in tying data back to ethics.

strengths: Appropriate number of supporting documents (4) directly referenced in analysis
Sources vary (financial statements, investigative journalism, market reports)
weaknesses: Depth of linkage to argument occasionally underdeveloped (some docs referenced descriptively)
Could include more primary or academic sources for broader perspective
Minor inconsistencies in how evidence is discussed across sections
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Four wellâchosen, relevant documents meet the 3â5 requirement and support the narrative (Band 3â4). While depth and breadth of perspectives are strong, occasional descriptive use rather than fully analytical treatment justifies a midâband score of 3.

strengths: SWOT tool is appropriately applied and linked to ethics
Basic quantitative statistics underpin the financial discussion
weaknesses: Very limited range of tools (only SWOT and simple statistics)
Lack of complementary models (e.g., Porterâs Five Forces, STEEPLE)
Methodological choices (quarter selection, GMV focus) are not justified
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Selection is narrow (âsome toolsâ) and application, while relevant, lacks justification and breadth. This aligns with Band 2 rather than higher bands requiring a more effective toolkit.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Adequate use of quantitative data (GMV, market share, marketing spend)
Attempts to balance profitability with ethical risks
Inclusion of primary research reflections in evaluation
weaknesses: Some data lack sourcing clarity and critical interrogation
Analysis occasionally remains at descriptive level
Quantitative and ethical strands are unevenly integrated
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates âsome analysisâ with adequate data selection (Band 3). Though it moves beyond mere description, arguments can be superficial and require deeper integration for the top bands.

strengths: Conclusions generally consistent with the presented evidence
Research question is explicitly restated and addressed
weaknesses: Judgement remains broad and lacks nuanced weighing of arguments
Ethical evaluation in the conclusion is underdeveloped
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Conclusions are present and aligned with evidence but are general and only partially justified, matching Band 2 criteria.

strengths: Logical sequence of sections (Introduction, SWOT, Financial analysis, Conclusion)
Clear headings and a table of contents guide the reader
weaknesses: Table of contents missing page numbers
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Overall organization is appropriate and consistent throughout, earning the top band (Band 2), despite minor formatting and navigational lapses.

strengths: All required IA elements are present (title, RQ, word count, declaration, references, documents)
weaknesses: Figure captions and footnotes do not conform to academic style
Redundant URLs and duplicated reference entries
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although no required elements are missing, significant inconsistencies in citations, reference list, and figure formatting detract from professionalism. These presentation flaws merit Band 1 under the rubric.

strandSelections.reasoning: The report follows a logical sequence (Introduction, SWOT, Financial analysis, Conclusion) and includes a table of contents.  Headings guide the reader, and referencing is placed at the end.  Minor formatting issues and occasional abrupt transitions exist, but overall organisation is appropriate, matching the top band for this criterion.

The sources are varied (financial statements, investigative journalism, market databases) and directly illuminate Temuâs marketing spend, GMV, labour accusations and privacy lawsuits.  Citations are embedded throughout the narrative, showing depth rather than token reference.  Together they offer multiple perspectives (financial, social, legal), providing a broad evidence base, so the top band for relevance and depth is warranted.

Required IA elementsâtitle, research question, word count, declaration, references and supporting documentsâare all present.  No compulsory component is missing, so the highest presentation band applies.

Four supporting documents are attached and clearly referenced (#1â#4).  This sits within the 3â5 range and each appears to be substantive (PDD financials, BBC article, ECDB industry report, FT investigation).  No superfluous or excessive documentation is provided, meeting the expectations of the middle-to-upper bands.  As the quantity criterion alone is judged here, Band 3 is appropriate.

SWOT is applied directly to Temuâs strategy and linked to ethical considerations; basic statistics support the financial discussion.  Relevance is generally clear, though the student does not always drive the analysis back to the research question (e.g., descriptive download trends lack ethical commentary).  This aligns with âadequately applied; relevance not always clear,â meriting Band 3.

The IA cites GMV figures, market-share percentages, marketing-expense changes and Statista download data, demonstrating an adequate (but not exhaustive) selection of quantitative evidence.  Data are used to illustrate points rather than merely listed, yet some numbers lack sourcing clarity or critical interrogation.  Hence "adequate" not "sufficient" or "effective."

The analysis moves beyond description (e.g., linking rising marketing costs to pressure on profitability and ethical concerns) and attempts to balance benefits with ethical risks.  Nevertheless, arguments are occasionally superficial, and integration of quantitative and ethical angles is uneven.  This situates the work in the âsome analysisâ category, Band 3.

The research question is explicitly restated and an answer is provided, but the wording "quite costly, does have the potential to be profitable, and in some aspects unethical" is general and not strongly justified.  This constitutes a partial, not comprehensive, responseâBand 2 fits best.

Tool selection is limited primarily to SWOT and simple descriptive statistics; no other business analytical models (e.g. Ansoff, STEEPLE, Porter) are used.  While SWOT is appropriate, the narrow repertoire reflects only "some" tools, placing the work in Band 2 rather than adequate or effective selection.

The conclusion references the main findings (costly yet growth-driving; labour/data concerns) and is broadly in line with the evidence presented, but it is concise and lacks nuanced weighing of arguments.  Thus it is "somewhat" consistent rather than fully substantiated, earning Band 2.

Temu is the sole focus of the work.  Specific evidenceâGMV growth, market-share percentages, marketing spend, privacy lawsuits, U.S. tariff proposalsâanchors every section to the organisation.  The link is more than descriptive; the student analyses how each factor shapes the ethical standing of Temuâs strategy.  Minor lapses (brief generic tool definitions) prevent a fully seamless integration, yet the connection remains strong and sustained.

The student selects ETHICS as the key concept and repeatedly revisits ethical dimensions (labour conditions, privacy breaches, tariff fairness) in the SWOT, financial analysis and conclusion. Ethical considerations are discussed alongside profitability, showing clear analytical thinking that links the concept to different business functions.  However, the discussion is still compartmentalised â a dedicated âFinancial analysisâ section drifts into profitability with limited ethical reflection, and quantitative data are not always tied back to the ethical lens.  The analysis is therefore strong and partially integrated, but not woven seamlessly âthroughoutâ the IA, so Band 5 is not fully justified.

annotations.comment: Research question is split across lines, which disrupts readability. Combine it into one concise statement and ensure it aligns exactly with the IA focus.
The student clearly selects âEthicsâ as the central theme, signaling a strong integration of the key concept throughout the assessment.
All required frontâmatter elements (title, RQ, word count, declaration) are present, meeting the presentation expectations.
The table of contents omits page numbers, making navigation difficult. Add corresponding page references for each section.
Introduction effectively situates Temuâs global context and uses multiple sources to anchor the narrative in real data.
Early in the introduction, explicitly tie the market context back to ethical risksâthis will strengthen the link to the key concept from the outset.
Annotate each SWOT entry to highlight its ethical implicationsâfor example, link âlow pricingâ directly to labor conditions.
The clear subsection headings in the SWOT (Strength, Weakness, etc.) enhance organization and reader navigation.
Footnote formatting is inconsistent (e.g., placement and style); standardize citation style for greater professionalism.
The SWOT definition remains generic and does not explicitly mention ethics. Integrate the chosen concept into the tool description.
The 1-cent pricing example is compelling; consider discussing the ethical cost implications for the supply chain to deepen analysis.
Linking gamification to underage user engagement shows strong ethical integration with marketing analysis.
Weakness claims about product quality lack data on frequency or scope of complaints. Add quantitative customer feedback or defect rates.
Discuss specific download milestones and quantify the decline in February 2024, rather than general statements, to enhance precision.
The CSR and labor exploitation critique is insightful; integrating quantitative labor data (e.g., wage comparisons) would solidify the argument.
The conclusion restates the main points but remains general. Develop a more nuanced judgement explicitly addressing the extent of ethicality.
The reference list contains inconsistencies in formatting (italics, punctuation). Apply one citation style uniformly across all entries.
Multiple URLs and repetitive details clutter the references. Remove duplication and ensure each source is listed once with complete details.
Identifying an 18% marketingâspend increase is good, but add a comparison to revenue growth or ROI to contextualize the ethical cost of acquisition.
Opportunity analysis uses solid market data but omits ethical evaluation of how inflation-driven demand could impact supply-chain labor.
The labor-exploitation threats section is thorough; consider referencing Temuâs CSR statements to present a balanced evaluation.
Separate privacy and labor concerns under distinct subheadings to improve clarity and emphasize each ethical dimension.
Clarify the methodology behind selecting these nine quarters and justify why GMV is the sole performance metric.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The dramatic GMV increase is well presented, but the narrative misses an ethical interpretation of how profitability drives or conflicts with labor practices.
Presenting GMV growth from $3M to $13.8B is impressive; calculate the CAGR to quantify growth rate and strengthen the analysis.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The $30 loss per order figure needs context on average order value or margin benchmarks to clarify its impact on profitability.
The download-trend graph is an appropriate visual and clearly labeled, effectively illustrating consumer uptake over time.
Reformat the figure caption to conform to academic style, e.g., âFigure 2. Monthly downloads of the Temu app in the US (Statista, 2024).â
Acknowledging primary research and comparative case studies in the reflection shows a thoughtful evaluation of limitations.
Future research suggestions lack explicit ethical dimensions; consider adding stakeholder interviews focused on ethical perceptions of Temu.
